description: BEACN DRep corrections log. Append-only and hash-chained: each entry's prev_sha256 is the sha256 of the previous entry's canonical JSON (keys sorted, separators ',' ':', ensure_ascii), so no past entry can be edited or removed without breaking the chain. Verified in-browser at #/corrections.
hash_rule: prev_sha256 = sha256(json.dumps(previous_entry, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":")))  # ensure_ascii; first entry: null

entries.title: Tweag NO anchor is internally contradictory
entries.what_happened: The rationale anchored in the NO vote on 'Tweag Core Cardano Infrastructure: Treasury Withdrawal 2026–2027' (cast 2026-06-19, tx c330223646e70fff90998c00529617adf8851b37a1e67ca75d0ff5797abd8c42) contradicts itself: the header records 'Recommendation: NO … Confidence: 1.0' while the plain-language explanation in the same document says BEACN is recording ABSTAIN because its chain snapshot was about eight hours old.
entries.blast_radius: One anchored rationale. The NO vote itself was the engine's directional output and stands; a reader of the anchored document sees two different verdicts and a confidence figure that measured penalty weight, not evidence coverage.
entries.root_cause: The plain-language explanation was precomputed from an earlier ABSTAIN-recommending run and not regenerated when the deterministic decision moved to NO before submission. Confidence at the time reported penalty size rather than verified evidence coverage (semantics corrected in beacn-drep-soul values_hierarchy, 2026-07-04).

entries.title: HF v11 and committeeMinSize YES rationales anchored with template text
entries.what_happened: The YES votes on 'Hard Fork to Protocol Version 11 (van Rossem)' (tx 19e6c5452ae9e8e0fdffc47eeafe28452443ec3220800033772b221c3aaa65c3) and 'Reduce the committeeMinSize parameter from 7 to 5' (tx 980a4f93c991b76b01149330cf873ef41d2dbef4e4bb0de4fd5c6c86d7f5c1f8) were cast 2026-06-17 while the model reasoning layer was unavailable. The anchored rationales carry fixed template explanations ('deterministic-template' / offline-review boilerplate) instead of a model-written one, and the hard-fork rationale records 'Proposal claims not extracted (model layer unavailable)'.
entries.blast_radius: Two anchored rationales on two on-chain YES votes. The votes were produced by the deterministic engine and are unaffected; the anchored explanations are thinner than the standard the system holds itself to.
entries.root_cause: The vote path did not require the reasoning layer to be reachable before anchoring; with it down, the deterministic fallback explanation was anchored as-is.

entries.title: Wrong live NO on 'Eternl: Path to Sustainability - v2' from dual id-spelling double-scoring
entries.what_happened: The same proposal arrived under both its CIP-129 '<tx>#<index>' id and its legacy bech32 'gov_action1…' id, and the engine scored each spelling separately. The evidence packets were keyed to one spelling, so the other scored with no evidence; the vote path only resolved the evidence-less spelling and BEACN cast a live on-chain NO against a proposal the evidenced run scored YES.
entries.blast_radius: One live mainnet vote in the wrong direction for roughly three weeks, plus double-counted entries inflating the visible action set.
entries.root_cause: No canonical action-id form at ingestion: two spellings of one action were treated as two actions, and the vote adapter's id decoder understood only the bech32 spelling.
entries.fix: Corrected on-chain NO→YES 2026-07-13, tx 53985b85c8fca6b413173642645bbda2c00c399aa86fd1ac78a8bfbfaf8e0957. Ids are now canonicalised at ingestion with field-wise merge in beacn-drep-core (src/beacn_drep/ids.py), regression-tested.

entries.title: Fee-only flow basis overstated the treasury outflow ratio (~1776)
entries.what_happened: Published treasury sustainability claims measured inflow from chain fees only, understating true treasury inflow by roughly 500x and pinning the withdrawal-to-inflow ratio near 1776 — which marked every treasury action 'unsustainable' on an untrue number.
entries.blast_radius: Every treasury rationale published on the fee-only basis carried the inflated ratio. Vote directions did not change on restatement: the honest ratio (~2.28) is still above the unchanged >2.0 threshold.
entries.root_cause: Inflow was derived from the fee pot alone instead of the full treasury credit (ada_pots treasury delta plus enacted withdrawals = tau share plus donations).
entries.fix: Restated to the true-inflow basis 2026-07-04: beacn-drep-soul treasury_spending_doctrine.json v1.2.0 (see the soul CHANGELOG entry of that date).

entries.title: treasury_summary.json mixed unlabeled bases (~500x misread)
entries.what_happened: treasury_summary.json published a fee-derived 6-month inflow figure beside a withdrawal ratio computed on the total-inflow basis, with neither basis labeled. A reader combining the two numbers misreads treasury inflow by roughly 500x.
entries.blast_radius: One published summary artifact. Decisions were unaffected — the engine scored on the true-inflow basis — but the public artifact invited a false conclusion.
entries.root_cause: Two different measurement bases shared one file with no per-number basis labels.
entries.fix: Superseded 2026-07-15 by budget_state.json, which labels every number's basis; treasury_summary.json now carries an inline deprecation naming its fee-derived basis.

entries.title: Publish outage 2026-07-12..15: stale anchor host, no automated votes
entries.what_happened: Three stacked failures: a GitHub SSH timeout on 2026-07-12, then the publisher refusing to push after the repository diverged from concurrent manual work, then a git worktree leaked by a crashed run blocking every later publish. gh-pages — the host the on-chain rationale anchors resolve to — was stale 2026-07-13..15 and no automated votes were cast 2026-07-13..15. This site's main branch kept publishing daily throughout.
entries.blast_radius: Freshness only: stale anchor host for about two days and missed vote runs. No vote was cast on stale data — the pipeline failed closed — and every already-cast vote remained valid and verifiable on-chain.
entries.root_cause: Publish and vote submission shared one failure domain; a crashed run could leave a stale worktree behind; the publisher assumed its checkout was on the origin tip.
entries.fix: Fixed 2026-07-15 in the pipeline: worktrees pruned at start of run, publish builds on the origin tip, and vote submission decoupled from publish so a publish failure cannot block voting.
